items.title: Housing shortage does not deter town from embracing migrants

children.content: The township of Naracoorte, nestled in South Australia's rural heartland, has put out the welcome mat for migrants, who have helped enrich its social fabric and boost the economy.

items.title: They didn't know what footy was doing to their boy. 'Now it's too late'

children.content: A talent and a team player, Nick Lowden excelled at Aussie rules. But the knocks, bumps, hits and collisions were adding up.

items.title: Butters coy about Power future after starring in upset Showdown win

children.content: Following a masterful display, Zak Butters is awarded the Showdown Medal in what may be his last match for the Power against the Crows.

image.altText: Zak Butters wearing the Showdown Medal.

items.title: 'Worst' wind lull pushes green poster child South Australia to its power limits

children.content: South Australia is at the bleeding edge of the energy transition but record low winds have pushed its grid to the brink of its limits.

image.altText: Two wind turbines stand side by side against a hazy blue sky

items.title: Volunteers say hotline told them to collect dead bird against government advice

children.content: South Australian citizen scientists say they called the Emergency Animal Disease Hotline to report a dead sea bird and were given advice that contradicts official government messaging.

image.altText: A dead pied cormorant in shallow beach water

items.title: The dry heart of Australia is now awash with water

children.content: The myth of an inland sea lured 19th century European explorers into the heart of Australia. But where they found only desert, this year it's awash, with water levels at Kati Thanda-Lake Eyre now the best some locals have ever seen.

articles.title: Heavy rain to soak Australia's east as un-El-Niño-like weather continues

children.content: For an El Niño year, 2026 is so far the wettest on record for Australia, and an influx of tropical moisture is about to deliver another round of widespread rain.

items.title: No-show cuttlefish result in million-dollar loss for regional SA town

children.content: Whyalla has been forced to wind back services and let staff let go as its tourism industry suffers million-dollar losses from cuttlefish not making their usual migration.

items.title: SA Police officers 'did not act appropriately' in handling of DV survivor's case

children.content: South Australia Police has admitted that three officers did not "act appropriately" in how they handled the case of domestic violence victim Stella Magnisalis, who was assaulted multiple times by her then police detective husband.

items.title: Death of man who held pregnant wife hostage 'not preventable', coroner finds

items.title: Vets turn away wild birds as avian influenza detected in SA

items.title: Antic's 'dangerous' comments on vaccinations condemned

items.title: Kangaroo Island declared feral pig-free after more than two centuries

children.content: Devastating bushfires provide Kangaroo Island with a unique silver lining as it becomes the largest island in the world to eradicate feral pigs.
